graphdb_rdf.metric_question: What types of spaces are explicitly stated as NOT being publicly accessible spaces?
graphdb_rdf.metric_answer: The types of spaces explicitly stated as NOT being publicly accessible spaces include: 1. **Prisons** - These are facilities where individuals are incarcerated and are not accessible to the general public. 2. **Border Control Areas** - These are locations related to the control and regulation of borders, which are also not open to the public. These exclusions are part of the definition of publicly accessible spaces as outlined in the EU AI Act.
